truth.papers.A61.title: Fatigue properties of Fe-30.5Mn-8Al-1C austenitic low-density steel: Critical impact of κ-carbide precipitation state
truth.papers.A61.paper_scope: Systematically investigates the tension–tension fatigue behavior and deformation mechanisms of solution-treated and aging-treated Fe-30.5Mn-8Al-1C (wt%) austenitic low-density steels, focusing on the critical role of κ-carbide precipitation state in controlling fatigue properties.

prediction.papers.A61.title: Fatigue properties of Fe-30.5Mn-8Al-1C austenitic low-density steel: Critical impact of κ-carbide precipitation state
prediction.papers.A61.paper_scope: Systematic investigation of tension-tension fatigue behavior and deformation mechanisms of solution-treated and aging-treated Fe-30.5Mn-8Al-1C austenitic low-density steel, focusing on the critical role of intragranular and intergranular κ-carbide precipitation states in controlling fatigue life, cyclic response, and damage mechanisms.

truth.processes.proc_fe30_5mn8al1c_st.descript: Forged and hot-rolled, then solution treated at 1050 °C for 1 h followed by water quenching (ST condition)
truth.processes.proc_fe30_5mn8al1c_550_6.descr: Solution treated at 1050 °C for 1 h + water quenching, then aged at 550 °C for 6 h (550-6 condition)
truth.processes.proc_fe30_5mn8al1c_600_6.descr: Solution treated at 1050 °C for 1 h + water quenching, then aged at 600 °C for 6 h (600-6 condition)
truth.processes.proc_fe30_5mn8al1c_650_6.descr: Solution treated at 1050 °C for 1 h + water quenching, then aged at 650 °C for 6 h (650-6 condition)

prediction.processes.proc_fe30_5mn8al1c_st.descript: Solution treatment at 1050 °C for 1 h followed by water quenching to room temperature; starting from a hot-rolled 4 mm sheet.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30_5mn8al1c_st.processe: The as-cast 50 kg ingot was forged into a 30 mm × 30 mm billet and then hot-rolled to 4 mm thickness prior to solution treatment.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30_5mn8al1c_550_6.descr: Aging at 550 °C for 6 h after solution treatment; produces high-density nanoscale intragranular κ-carbides (~11 nm) with rectangular morphology uniformly precipitated within austenite grains.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30_5mn8al1c_550_6.proce: Selected ST samples were further aged to tailor κ-carbide precipitation state.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30_5mn8al1c_600_6.descr: Aging at 600 °C for 6 h after solution treatment; produces coarsened intragranular κ-carbides (~18 nm) forming rectangular parallelepiped arrays along orthogonal <100> directions, with concurrent intergranular κ-carbide precipitation.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30_5mn8al1c_650_6.descr: Aging at 650 °C for 6 h after solution treatment; leads to further coarsening of intragranular κ-carbides (~24 nm) and more pronounced intergranular κ-carbide precipitation.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_st: Water quenching to room temperature; condition designated as ST.

truth.structures.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_st.overa: Fully austenitic microstructure with no κ-carbides; short-range ordering present.
truth.structures.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_550_6.ov: Austenitic matrix with uniform nanoscale intragranular κ-carbides; no evident intergranular κ-carbides.
truth.structures.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_550_6.mi: nanoscale rectangular precipitates, uniform intragranular distribution
truth.structures.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_600_6.ov: Austenitic matrix with coarsened intragranular κ-carbides and minor intergranular κ-carbide precipitation.
truth.structures.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_600_6.mi: uniform intragranular, with some intergranular precipitation
truth.structures.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_650_6.ov: Austenitic matrix with coarsened intragranular κ-carbides and severe intergranular κ-carbide precipitation.
truth.structures.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_650_6.mi: intragranular, with pronounced intergranular κ-carbide precipitation
coherent (intragranular), possibly semi-coherent/incoherent at grain boundaries

prediction.structures.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_550_6.ov: Austenitic matrix with uniform nanoscale intragranular κ-carbide precipitation
prediction.structures.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_600_6.ov: Austenitic matrix with intragranular κ-carbide arrays and intergranular κ-carbide precipitation
prediction.structures.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_600_6.mi: rectangular parallelepipeds forming well-defined arrays aligned along orthogonal <100> matrix directions
prediction.structures.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_650_6.ov: Austenitic matrix with coarsened intragranular and pronounced intergranular κ-carbide precipitation
prediction.structures.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_650_6.mi: pronounced precipitation at grain boundaries disrupting grain boundary continuity

truth.interfaces.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_550_6.in: Intragranular κ-carbides promote planar slip bands and homogeneous strain accommodation.
truth.interfaces.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_600_6.in: Minor intergranular κ-carbides begin to cause local stress concentrations at grain boundaries.
truth.interfaces.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_600_6.ph: Coarsening of intragranular κ-carbides and onset of intergranular precipitation.
truth.interfaces.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_650_6.de: Coarse intergranular κ-carbides act as strong slip barriers, intensifying dislocation pile-ups and stress concentration, leading to premature crack nucleation.
truth.interfaces.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_650_6.in: Severe strain localization at grain boundaries decorated with intergranular κ-carbides.
truth.interfaces.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_650_6.ph: Severe intergranular κ-carbide precipitation degrades grain boundary cohesion.

prediction.interfaces.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_550_6.de: Moving dislocations shear coherent precipitates creating anti-phase boundaries; repeated shearing causes mechanical dissolution during cyclic loading
prediction.interfaces.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_600_6.de: Intragranular κ-carbides impede dislocation movement and redistribute local crack-tip stress field, promoting activation of multiple slip systems
prediction.interfaces.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_650_6.de: Coarse intergranular κ-carbides act as strong slip barriers, intensifying dislocation pile-ups, elevating local stress concentration, and promoting earlier crack nucleation
prediction.interfaces.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_650_6.ph: Severe intergranular precipitation undermines beneficial intragranular strengthening effect and degrades grain boundary integrity

truth.properties.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_st.mecha: Tensile engineering stress–strain curves shown in Fig. 4.
truth.properties.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_550_6.me: Tensile engineering stress–strain curves shown in Fig. 4.
truth.properties.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_600_6.me: Tensile engineering stress–strain curves shown in Fig. 4.
truth.properties.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_650_6.me: Tensile engineering stress–strain curves shown in Fig. 4.

prediction.performance.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_600_6.s: stress-controlled tension-tension cyclic loading, sinusoidal waveform, 20 Hz, stress ratio R = 0.1, ambient air
prediction.performance.sample_fe30_5mn8al1c_600_6.l: Basquin equation: Δσ/2 = σ'f (2Nf)^b fitted to finite-life fatigue data

truth.characterization_methods.sample_fe30_5mn: Fractographic analysis and surface deformation features

Fracture surfaces show crack initiation, propagation, and rapid fracture zones; fatigue striations spacing correlates with fatigue life. ST sample: dimples. 650-6 sample: mixed dimples and cleavage facets.
Characterize κ-carbide precipitation and deformation substructures
550-6: ~11 nm intragranular κ-carbides, no intergranular precipitates; planar slip bands dominate.
Characterize κ-carbide evolution and deformation mechanisms
600-6: ~18 nm κ-carbides with ordered arrays, minor intergranular precipitation. Uniform planar slip bands with high reversibility.
Examine κ-carbide coarsening and dislocation-precipitate interactions
650-6: ~24 nm κ-carbides, severe intergranular precipitation; dislocation pile-ups at grain boundaries; mechanical dissolution of κ-carbides by repeated shearing.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe30_5mn: EBSD on TESCAN MIRA3 SEM with Oxford detector
Microstructural characterization of undeformed sample and fatigue crack propagation path analysis
Undeformed ST sample exhibits fully austenitic FCC microstructure (IPF map); fatigued sample at 225 MPa shows relatively straight transgranular crack path along slip bands in large austenite grains
TEM on JEOL JEM-2100F at 200 kV
κ-carbide precipitation characterization and post-fatigue deformation microstructure analysis

High-density nanoscale intragranular κ-carbides (~11 nm) with rectangular morphology uniformly precipitated within austenite grains; fatigued sample shows uniformly distributed planar slip bands with low dislocation density; dislocation shearing of κ-carbides creating anti-phase boundaries observed by HRTEM/IFFT at 180 MPa
EBSD band contrast and IPF superimposed maps, KAM maps, and SF maps
Fatigue crack propagation path and local strain field analysis at 225 MPa

Increased crack-path tortuosity and microbranching compared with ST; more moderate KAM levels with diffuse misorientation distribution around crack tip, indicating homogeneous plastic accommodation by uniformly dispersed intragranular κ-carbides; cracks nucleate in low-Schmid-factor grains and propagate into high-Schmid-factor grains
Fracture surface morphology examination in rapid fracture zone after fatigue
Mixed fracture characteristics featuring both dimples and cleavage faceted regions at final fracture stage, associated with reduced ductility due to extensive intergranular κ-carbide precipitation

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Fitted to experimental Δσ/2-2Nf curves with root mean square error of log10 Nf reported in Table 1
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Finite-life fatigue analysis of stress-controlled tension-tension tests
Δσ/2 = σ'f (2Nf)^b, where Δσ/2 is stress amplitude, Nf is fatigue life, σ'f is fatigue strength coefficient, b is fatigue strength exponent
Linear in double-logarithmic coordinates; fitted parameters for ST, 550-6, 600-6, and 650-6 are reported in Table 1
prediction.computational_details.idx_1.analytical_m: Estimation of strengthening increments from intragranular κ-carbides in the shearing regime

Δσ_cs = KM(Gεc)^(3/2)(rf/0.5Gb)^(1/2); Δσ_ms = 0.0055M(ΔG)^(3/2)(2f/G)^(1/2)(r/b)^(3m/2-1); Δσ_os = 0.81M(γ_APB/2b)(3πf/8)^(1/2). Constants K=2.6 and m=0.85. M is Taylor factor, εc is lattice misfit strain, b is Burgers vector, G is shear modulus, ΔG is modulus misfit, γ_APB is anti-phase boundary energy, r and f are radius and volume fraction of intragranular κ-carbides.

Used to rationalize non-monotonic fatigue strength ranking based on precipitate size; coherency strain, modulus mismatch, and order strengthening contributions increase with precipitate size at fixed volume fraction

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Fatigue crack propagation zone area fractions (fa) were measured on SEM images and labelled in Fig. 6; numerical values are not given in the text.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: EBSD step size used for crack path analysis was 0.8 μm.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Precipitation strengthening contributions (Δσ_cs, Δσ_ms, Δσ_os) are modelled but not numerically quantified for these conditions.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Fatigue tests were performed under stress control with a sinusoidal waveform at 20 Hz, stress ratio R=0.1.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: The dominant deformation mechanism in all samples is planar dislocation slip; in ST it is associated with short-range ordering, while in aged samples it is enhanced by κ-carbide shearing (glide plane softening).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: A critical κ-carbide size for transition from shearing to Orowan bypassing is reported as ~27 nm based on literature; all samples here remain in the shearing regime.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: No quantitative data on density, hardness, or thermal/electrical properties were reported.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Figure 5 displays Δσ/2-2Nf curves; exact data points are not extractable from the text.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: The fatigue strength improvement percentages relative to ST were calculated as approximately 2.9% (550-6), 11.4% (600-6), and 8.6% (650-6) based on stress amplitudes at 10^7 cycles.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Solution treatment was conducted on a hot-rolled 4 mm sheet obtained by forging a 30 mm × 30 mm billet from a 50 kg vacuum-melted ingot.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Tensile tests: room temperature, constant strain rate of 10^-3 s^-1, tensile axis parallel to rolling direction, Shimadzu AG-Xplus 250 kN.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Fatigue tests: stress-controlled tension-tension, sinusoidal waveform, 20 Hz, stress ratio R = 0.1, ambient air, Shimadzu EHF-EV050K2A-020-1, loading axis parallel to rolling direction.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Table 1 provides uniform elongation (UE) and total elongation (TE); UE values are 57.7 ± 1.3 % (ST), 45.5 ± 1.4 % (550-6), 45.1 ± 1.5 % (600-6), 46.4 ± 1.2 % (650-6); TE values are 72.0 ± 0.8 %, 57.7 ± 0.4 %, 58.4 ± 1.4 %, 52.7 ± 1.6 %, respectively.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Fig. 4 tabulates engineering stress values (MPa) at engineering strains of 0 %, 20 %, 40 %, 60 %, and 70 % for all four samples, but continuous stress-strain curve data are not provided in text.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Fig. 5 presents Δσ/2-2Nf curves in double-logarithmic coordinates; fatigue strengths at 10^7 cycles are 157.5 MPa (ST), 162 MPa (550-6), 175.5 MPa (600-6), and 171 MPa (650-6).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Fig. 6 macroscopic fracture surfaces show three-zone morphology (crack initiation, propagation, rapid fracture); the area fraction of crack initiation and propagation zones is larger in aged samples than in ST, with 600-6 largest at 180 MPa and 550-6 highest at 315 MPa, consistent with optimal fatigue resistance at low and high stress amplitudes respectively.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Fig. 7 fatigue crack propagation zones exhibit well-defined fatigue striations indicating transgranular crack advancement; 550-6 and 600-6 show more pronounced and closely spaced striations, with narrowest spacing in 550-6 at 315 MPa and in 600-6 at 180 MPa.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Fig. 8 rapid fracture zones: ST, 550-6, and 600-6 show dimples indicating ductile microvoid coalescence; 650-6 displays mixed dimples and cleavage facets attributed to extensive intergranular κ-carbide precipitation.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_10.value: Fig. 9 EBSD analysis at 225 MPa reveals transgranular crack propagation along slip bands; KAM maps show highest grain-boundary strain localization in ST and localized hot spots at intergranular κ-carbides in 650-6; SF maps confirm cracks nucleate in hard-oriented (low SF) grains and propagate into soft-oriented (high SF) grains.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_11.value: Fig. 10 surface deformation morphologies: ST shows wavy slip (cross slip) at 180 MPa and kink bands at 315 MPa; 600-6 exhibits distinct planar slip bands at both amplitudes due to glide plane softening, with decreased band spacing and cooperative multiple slip activation at 315 MPa.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_12.value: Fig. 11 post-fatigue TEM deformation microstructures: ST exhibits persistent Lüder's bands (PLBs) at 180 MPa indicating strain localization; aged samples show lower overall dislocation density and more uniformly distributed planar slip bands, with narrower spacing at 315 MPa.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_13.value: Fig. 14 TEM near grain boundaries at 315 MPa: 550-6 shows uniform intragranular κ-carbides facilitating organized planar slip transfer across grain boundaries; 650-6 shows coarse intergranular κ-carbides disrupting grain boundary continuity, impeding slip transfer and causing severe dislocation pile-ups and wavy-slip patterns.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_14.value: The critical precipitate size for shearing-to-Orowan transition in analogous Fe-Mn-Al-C alloy systems is reported as approximately 27 nm; all investigated aged conditions (11–24 nm intragranular κ-carbides) remain in the shearing regime.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_15.value: The 600-6 sample exhibits the best overall mechanical performance among investigated conditions, combining high tensile strength with superior fatigue strength and fatigue life.
